title: Writing the equation of a line from gradient and y-intercept
problem: Write the equation of a line with gradient $m = 3$ and y-intercept $b = -4$.

steps.instruction: Write the general equation of a straight line.
Substitute the gradient $m = 3$ and the y-intercept $b = -4$.

title: Finding the equation of a line from two points
problem: Find the equation of the line passing through $(1, 2)$ and $(4, 11)$.

steps.instruction: Find the gradient using the gradient formula.
Write the equation using $m = 3$, then substitute one point to find $b$.
Write the equation of the line.

title: Finding the equation of a line parallel to a given line
problem: Find the equation of the line that is parallel to $y = 2x - 3$ and passes through the point $(4, 11)$.

steps.instruction: Identify the gradient of the parallel line. Parallel lines have equal gradients.
steps.working: The line $y = 2x - 3$ has gradient $m = 2$, so the new line also has $m = 2$.

steps.instruction: Write the equation of the new line with $m = 2$ and an unknown y-intercept.
Substitute the given point $(4, 11)$ to find $b$.
Write the equation of the line.
